radio_text:
Thank you for tuning in to KPTN 89.3 FM, New Hampton's Public Radio. I'm Grace Spivack and we're now broadcasting on a taped loop, which we will update daily. The offices of WNHP, located on the top floor of the Romero Towers downtown, are equipped with backup generators which have allowed us to maintain radio contact with the outside world. Normal mobile phone communications have been disabled, likely due to an explosion at the New Hampton Power Exchange, which we believe sent a massive power surge into the island's grid. Many local businesses and individuals have reported power outages as well.

Official word on the current emergency has been slow to come, but WNHP has confirmed that the White House is aware of the disaster and is sending a special team of investigators to decide the best course of action.

The emergency is mainly defined by a strange phenomenon that seems to have reanimated the corpses of the dead. KPTN had sent emails to researchers who might be able to clarify the cause of the apparent

of the recently deceased and their victims, but due to the current power outage, network communications are not possible. We await confirmation that, indeed, the dead have risen and are attacking the living, although by all accounts that appears to be what is happening.

In the meantime, listeners are recommended to stay put in their homes if possible. Barricade doors and windows in order to keep out the zombies. Zombies appear to be attracted to loud noises and the smell of open wounds, so listeners are advised to remain calm and quiet, preferably on an upper floor.

KPTN will update this recording as new information becomes available. This is Grace Spivack, good luck.

Hello again, from the studio of KPTN 89.3 FM. This is Grace Spivack, and I'm thankful to be with you again on the second day of this terrifying emergency.

The dead have risen. Dr. James Whitcombe of New Hampton General Hospital has confirmed that at the outset of the zombie outbreak recently deceased patients from the hospital morgue arose from their slabs and attacked hospital staff. The renegade corpses were detained in the morgue itself, but not before they had killed an orderly and wounded two other doctors.

The hospital staff has barricaded the doors and is no longer accepting new patients. Dr. Whitcombe describes the harrowing scene of a zombie mob pressing in on the hospital's doors.

Dr. Whitcombe also confirmed that once bitten an individual would become infected with what appears to be some kind of zombie virus. The virus can take hold in as little as twelve hours after infection, and leads to the death then reanimation of the infected individual.

Dr. Whitcombe is hopeful that a mass evacuation of remaining citizens will begin shortly, and stresses the importance of immediate action on the part of the government's emergency management agencies.

So far no further word has been received on the federal response to this crisis. Listeners are still advised to remain sheltered and not attempt movement in the streets. A reported explosion has destroyed the Mainland Bridge, making it very difficult to get off the island. 

Reports have also come in of large crowds gathered at the ferry docks and in the stadium, but KPTN could not get in touch with anyone at those locations. The danger of moving amidst the walking dead is to great to risk the life of an investigative team.

You have been listening to KPTN, 89.3 FM, New Hampton Public Radio. I'm Grace Spivack. Good night, and good luck.

WhoMP 103.3 Listeners, listen up! This is Madman Zack and in case you haven't noticed, the dead have risen. The Madman and the morning crew have sealed up the WHMP 103.3 FM studios and we'll keep broadcasting as long as we can. We've got a tape loop going so we can dedicate ourselves to barricading this joint. 

What we know so far is this: We’ve been getting some calls saying that there was some kind of explosion on the Mainland bridge.  Callers are saying that its epic, mass, fucking chaos. Cars backed up, cars on fire, people jumping into the river and—and I don’t know how to say this folks—a crowd of zombies coming after them.

It is no bullshit, listeners, and not the police or the FCC can save us from this! It's the god damned apocalypse. Granny, get your gun, and aim for the head. Hell has come to New Hampton!

Land lines are out, but last we heard people were heading to the stadium, the ferry house, and the east shore warehouse district. If you’re like me and only have a little life left on your cell phone call someone.  Call anyone and get to a safe place for the night.  If you can't get to one of these places, you might just want to barricade your windows and collect some fresh water.

Check back with the Madman later for more details as we get them.

This is Madman Zack and we're into day two of the WhoMP 103.3 FM Zombie Party. Let me tell you something, listeners, these things are dumb as hell. Yesterday, Lenny the Intern shot one sixteen times with a BB gun in the ass before it realized it was getting shot!

Here's what we know: The Feds are dragging ass getting us any help out here. WHMP is located right outside the city on the top of a hill, so we haven't seen a lot of walk-by traffic, zombie or otherwise. We sure as hell haven't seen any help coming our way. Once or twice the Madman might have heard a chopper overhead, but it's hard to tell over the sound of the generators. And why do we need generators? Because the damn power is still off!

The Morning Road-Rage Crew still hasn't made it back to the station, and the JamLine is completely down. Not much news is getting out, but we have heard that the ferry is actually running from the mainland to the island. Right now that looks like the best way to get out of here.

Listen folks, we still don’t know what’s causing this shit to go down so your best bet is to leave Dodge-—grab a plane, take the ferry, swim for it do what you can to save yourself because it doesn’t look like help is coming anytime soon.

But Madman Zack has never been worried about doing the “smart” thing. Madman Zack is staying put. I'll be damned if those zombies are gonna come and take my radio station. So I'll keep broadcasting for you. You keep listening.

It’s hellishly quiet out there today, folks.  The Madman reporting to you that there's not much activity going on.  I see some loose zombies stumbling about but this time I told our interns NOT to shoot and they walked right by us.  No brain surgeons amongst the zombie hordes if you catch my drift.  Stay tuned to Mad Zack all day every day to keep you posted and safe.

Mad Zack here; I’ve got a success story to share with the listeners of 103.3.  Early this morning there was a knock at our door.  Lenny thought it might be fucking one of them, but it wasn't.  It was Justin.  He’s the bravest six year old I’ve ever met.  Justin was on his way to school when we were attacked by these god damned monsters.  Little Justin Shwartz ran and hid for an entire day as he had to watch these monsters roam the streets for blood.  Justin, like the little soldier he is, knew that he couldn’t stay hidden for too long and made a break for it and went towards his school but no one was there.  But Justin didn’t give up!  He found food in the cafeteria at his school.  He turned on the radio and it was tuned to the one and only WHMP 103.3.  He heard me, Mad Zack, blasting out help to those of us struggling to survive this attack.  Listen to this folks!  Listen to have brave and smart little Justin is.  He knew where the WHMP studios are located because of our neon sign.  Little Justin, braving those monsters out there, sneaked all the way to our studio and saved himself.  He’s with us now in the WHMP studios safe and sound.  Yeah, bring him over here Len.  Yeah, give him the mic.  Justin why don’t you say something to all of those people listening out there.  Give them all some hope in this messy situation...

Don’t forget loyal listeners, little Justin stayed strong.  We all can learn from his story.

From the WHMP studio atop New Hampton Heights Hill I’m watching these fucking beasts congregate and move in packs.  Where there one there’s many, folks—so stay alert.  Better yet, stay indoors!

Gooooooooooooooooood mooooooorning, Viet Nam!  This is your big bad voodoo daddy, Mad Zack staying strong four days long and gonna be stronger longer than any other brother!  I'm coming off a solid eight hours of beauty rest.  Intern Lenny held down the fort last night and now I'm back and ready to open a can of whoop-ass!  Remember, with those monsters roaming around outside you need allies who you can trust.  It's us against them, baby!  And, we ain't gonna lose!  Boo-yah!

Don’t forget loyal listeners, little Justin stayed strong.  We all can learn from his story.

Mad Zack here, just thinking a little about these monsters we're up against.  Watching them from atop inside the station these fucking things aren't even human.  Sure they look like us.  Ten fingers.  Two eyes.  They look fucking humans...but that's their biggest strength: that they look like us.  But they're not us!  Listen to Mad Zack when he says this: They are not us.  They are not like us.  They're different from us and we can't afford to show them the respect and concern that we would show one of the living.  Listeners, you've got to fucking shoot first and ask the questions later if you want to survive.

WHMP 103.3 with Mad Zack on the mic and Intern Lenny on the board with our station's new and improved mascot, Little Justin Shwartz; good morning New Hamptonites.  Stay sharp and stay focused.  We haven't received any word from authorities yet but if we do, you'll be the first to know.  In the meantime, my advice is stay indoors and barricade yourselves in just like we have here at WHMP studios.

WHMP's Mad Zack with an important message to all of our listeners out there in New Hampton.  We've got a lot of people knocking on our doors down stairs.  Listen, people...these monsters mimic the living.  They groan, they make noise, they bang on shit.  I know that we opened the door for Justin and all, but Len, me, and Pete took a vote and we unanimously decided that we can't open the door anymore; so you've got to stop banging on our doors.  They're barricaded pretty tight and honestly, I think that all of the banging is causing more and more of those fucking zombies to come so you're doing everyone of New Hampton a diservice by bringing attention to us and our station.

Listen you fucking assholes, I've told you fucking once already.  Get the hell off this fucking property before you get more of those monsters on our fucking asses.  If we have to defend ourselves against yous we will.  Leave us fucking alone...for fucking god's sake, we've got a little kid in here!

Len, am I on?  Cool.  Listen up WHMP listeners, Mad Zack here and we've got a fucking problem on our hands here at the studio.  Last night, at about 3 or so in the morning a group of seven or so people tried to break in.  Now I know if you've listening for the past three or so days you know that this has been an issue of concern for us all here because if these fucking people stand ouside and try to get into the studios, even though there's no fucking room or food or anything for them here they're going to attract more and more zombies...but, at like 3 AM or so they tried to break in but we were able to drive them away--even though one of them, I think, has a gun.  Now Pete's downstairs guarding with fucking jury-rigged weapons.  Problem is, right now, even as I speak, they're downstairs trying to pry open our back door even though its pretty fortified.  You see, but that's where the bulk of our generators are so if we cut out at any point during the day you know that it'll be because of these fucking assholes trying to--

This is Mad Zack...I--I don't know how to say this but--those fucking monsters killed--they--they killed Justin.  They shot fucking Justin!  These fucking seven guys, one of 'em with a gun, tried to break in yesterday and after we held them off this fucking animal fired a warning round.  The bullet--it went through the celing and Justin was in the room above us.  We told him to stay there and hide behind boxes because it would be safe.  He let out this scream.  It was so, I--I..I can't do this, I'm sorry I just--

This is Pete.  I used to be the producer of the Afternoon Rock Out here on WHMP, before all of this went down.  Zackary's laying down for a bit but I promised him that I'd keep the airwaves trafficed so--in between us refortifying the studios...which is going to take a few days because the raid yesterday did some really bad damage to the south wall...I'm going to give all of our listeners there a head's up on any major zombie movement or anything else worth noting.  Nothing of note though yet, so I'm going to go back down and keep on working with Len.  This is Pete signing off for a bit from the WHMP studios.
